# ghost_settings (Resource)

Manages Ghost site settings (title, description, locale, timezone, SEO metadata, and social handles) via `PUT /ghost/api/admin/settings/`.

There is exactly one settings resource per Ghost instance. Use `terraform import ghost_settings.main _` to import.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "ghost_settings" "main" {
  title       = "My Blog"
  description = "Thoughts on things"
  lang        = "en"
  timezone    = "Europe/London"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `title` (String) Publication title.

### Optional

- `description` (String) Short publication description shown in meta tags and the Ghost Admin.
- `facebook` (String) Facebook page name (e.g. `ghost`).
- `lang` (String) Site language code (e.g. `en`, `de`, `fr`). Defaults to `en`.
- `meta_description` (String) SEO meta description override for the homepage.
- `meta_title` (String) SEO meta title override for the homepage.
- `timezone` (String) IANA timezone identifier (e.g. `Europe/London`). Defaults to `Etc/UTC`.
- `twitter` (String) Twitter/X handle (e.g. `@ghost`).

## Import

Import using the placeholder `_` (there is only one settings resource per Ghost instance):

```shell
terraform import ghost_settings.main _
```
